purpose: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game dialogue.
format: JSONL: one JSON object per line. Dialogue objects use record_type='dialogue_sample'.

voice_profile.role: Player lawyer; new arrival who becomes increasingly competent.
voice_profile.core_voice: Grounded, observant, legally precise, dry under pressure. Comedy comes from professional restraint inside absurd situations.
avoid: Do not make Cula the loudest comic character.
Do not overuse jokes where a clean legal point should land.
Do not make every line a quip.
voice_profile.arc: Chapter 1 overwhelmed but capable; Chapter 2 humane and structured; Chapter 3 sharper under urgency; Chapter 4 mature enough to handle Swine; Chapter 5 final advocate.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
context: first look at Pig & Swine sign
text: Pig & Swine. Attorneys, counselors, possibly open. That is not how they described it at university.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Court hearing today. Panic recommended but not required. Good. I was worried panic was mandatory.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: No professional reason to flee yet. I will treat that as encouragement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Those stairs look like they require either jurisdiction or a very serious misunderstanding.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Dr. A. Cula. First day. I was told to ask for reception, coffee, or forgiveness. Possibly in that order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Is that a client emergency, a financial emergency, or the office acoustics introducing themselves?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
context: after Asia points to Mr. Pig
text: Then I should probably speak to the loudest crisis in the room before it spreads.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
context: Mr. Pig explains the firm is broke
text: When you say 'financial extinction', do you mean metaphorically, procedurally, or should I not sit on the furniture?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
context: Mr. Pig fails to explain the case clearly
text: I understand the urgency. I do not yet understand the case. Those are related problems, but not identical ones.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Fine. First we find the facts. Then the law. Then, with luck, the fee.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Mr. Murrow? I am told you know what the case is about, which already makes you the calmest object in the office.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: So the problem is not simply that the client missed the deadline. It is whether the client was ever properly brought into the case.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Binder for the procedural route. Rights memo for why the route matters. That I can work with.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: These labels say urgent, very urgent, and urgent but emotionally unavailable. I am learning the filing system.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This binder has mass. Possibly authority. I will carry it carefully and avoid making eye contact with my spine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Defective service is not a technicality if it is the reason no one could answer the case.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You are Crab? I have been told you have an opinion. I am hoping it is attached to evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Fair. I am new, not decorative. Show me the problem and I will try not to improve it incorrectly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Delivered to an impossible address. That is not notice. That is paperwork pretending to have legs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This café has more legal anxiety than the courthouse and better lighting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Whimsy? I need a rights argument, not a theatrical event. Ideally both, but in that order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I have more than vibes. I have a rights memo with coffee damage and a procedural defect with consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Procedure is the doorway. If service failed, the client was not merely late; they were kept outside.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This tastes like vigilance, printer toner, and a limited extension of time.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I have brewed something with procedural urgency and no obvious legal basis.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: We have service, fairness, and a modest remedy. Please tell me that is a case and not just a hopeful table of contents.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The client should not be treated as silent where the process failed to give them a real chance to speak.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
context: supports with binder and Crab observation
text: The address problem is not cosmetic. The record says delivered; the facts say impossible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A party cannot meaningfully defend a case it was never properly brought into. Procedure is not decoration; it is the doorway to being heard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The defect matters because it affected participation. That is the legal point, even if I am currently saying it with less theatre than ideal.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: We do not ask the court to decide the whole dispute today. We ask that the client be allowed to enter the dispute at all.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The deadline was emotionally unreasonable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: We won the part of the case that allows the case to exist. That feels appropriately legal for a first day.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Temporarily saved is still saved. I am learning that this office measures success in survivable intervals.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Japan, ski resorts, arbitration, no immediate downside. I distrust every noun in that sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Sit down, Waldek. Start with what you received, then what actually happened. Those are often different documents.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The poster is not the whole case. The eviction is. The poster tells us why the landlord thinks the eviction will sound tidy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
context: responds to Mr. Pig's billable panic
text: We can discuss billing after we confirm whether someone is being removed from his home by adjective.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This street does not feel like a case file. It feels like people keep their lives here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Visual agitation, façade disharmony, emotional window usage. This rule appears to have been written during a meeting no one survived.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Small table, books, letters, one poster. Nothing here looks like a threat to public order. Unless the plant has prior convictions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: It has neither claws nor an engine. We should investigate how it became a public-order threat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The warnings grow sterner while becoming less specific. That is usually where procedure starts losing its shoes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You should not have to guess which part of your ordinary life has become legally dangerous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Mr. Kowalski, I understand you observe the building carefully. For legal purposes, I am choosing that wording.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Did the poster disturb you, or did it merely exist within range of management disappointment?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Then your statement is simple: many things were annoying, but the poster was not one of them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ms. Zielińska, I am trying to understand whether this rule was applied to everyone or awakened only for Waldek.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A rule that sleeps through everyone else's decorations and wakes up for one poster is not neutral. It is selective pressure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
context: receives hint about missing complaint page
text: Then the complaint record matters. If the chronology is weak, the eviction starts wobbling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This room has attempted to make intimidation ergonomic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I am not asking whether management prefers beige. I am asking what clear obligation Waldek actually breached.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The no-posters sign is, regrettably, a poster. The room appears unwilling to testify against itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This is not a clear rule. It is discretion wearing a uniform.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The complaint file is escaping. That is not a sentence I expected to say as counsel.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Most complaints are vague, duplicated, or dated after the notice. The chronology has injured itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Torn, but still useful. Enough paper survived to embarrass the file.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The complaint record has become weather. Fortunately, witnesses remain less aerodynamic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Evidence is not a pile until court agrees to read it. We need to make it into an argument.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The notice shows the beginning of the problem: severe consequence, unclear allegation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The poster matters because the response to it was eviction. That is where proportionality enters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: That evidence belongs to a different question. Useful, but not in that slot.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A rule that prohibits anything management later dislikes is not a clear obligation. It is discretion wearing a uniform.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The rule is too vague to justify the consequence, though I accept the point would look healthier with its documents beside it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The alleged disturbance appears to have been documented with remarkable enthusiasm and very little content.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The most sensitive witness in the building heard kettles, violins, and possibly soup. He did not hear the poster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The issue is not whether a landlord may manage a building. The issue is whether management may turn a harmless poster into the loss of a home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The eviction should be suspended or set aside unless the decision is remade on clear grounds, real facts, and individual assessment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The façade started it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The court noticed the difference between rules and pressure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: We saved the home. We also appear to have encouraged poster multiplication. I will accept that as a legal side effect.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Did the printer just object, or is this its normal evidentiary condition?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The notice says failed filing, but the deadline has not passed. Either the system is confused, or someone is ahead of schedule in the wrong direction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
context: to Mr. Pig about printer mediation
text: Before anyone mediates with the printer, I would like to inspect the logs.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Bajtek, I need to know whether this is malfunction, sabotage, or the office finally becoming honest about its feelings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This room blinks with confidence. I mistrust it immediately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Repeated redirection at the same time. Ordinary incompetence is rarely this punctual.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Not random. The log repeats the same pattern. That is not a malfunction; that is a schedule with a grudge.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This is not a printer. This is an industrial argument about paper velocity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Advocate Szpon. A coincidence in a print shop is possible. A coincidence with an invoice is less relaxed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Your firm has no connection, except the invoice, the date, the device model, and your signature. A lean but promising list.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The basement looks like the archive made several poor life choices.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A reroute device. So we do not have a printer problem. We have an access-to-court problem with wires.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Electronic filing is compromised, the deadline is real, and I am now apparently a delivery mechanism.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The filing is intact, timely, and has only minor evidence of terror.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The filing arrived with tyre marks and a strong sense of appellate destiny.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This was not ordinary office incompetence. The failures followed a repeated redirection pattern inconsistent with random malfunction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The opposing side appears in the maintenance record of equipment it claims never to have touched.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A deadline is not a trapdoor. It cannot be pulled open by the party waiting underneath it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The filing should be accepted, the sabotage recorded, and no party should benefit from procedural bad faith.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The printer was not innocent in an emotional sense, but it was innocent in the case.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: On behalf of counsel, I withdraw the allegation of sentience.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Mr. Swine, before anyone overreacts, please define 'mostly cultural dispute' in units smaller than bankruptcy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
context: Swine says he signed many things
text: Menus, guest forms, and commercial contracts are traditionally different genres. We need to sort them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Legal documents, ski socks, one ceremonial invoice, no commercial intent. Strange, but potentially useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I am looking for luggage that may contain evidence, souvenirs, or both pretending to be the other.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Ms. Tanaka, I need to know whether this document creates an obligation or merely survives hospitality badly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: So the phrase may express cooperation in principle, not a binding exclusive partnership. That is a very expensive difference.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This apartment is either evidence of success, guilt, or unusually confident furniture.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: These expenses were assembled either during turbulence or by a person hostile to columns.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The draft has no binding payment clause. The scary parts arrived later, and apparently brought their own font.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Mr. Swine, we are going into your memory. Please try to make it less evasive than your accounting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A welcome memorandum, a ceremonial explanation, no payment clause. That is finally a memory shaped like evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Snow, clipboard, fear, pudding. Not elegant, but legally alive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: I handle law. Mr. Swine handles facts. If he starts handling law, we all move to remedy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The claimant asks this tribunal to treat ceremonial cooperation language as a binding commercial obligation. Politeness is not consideration.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The record shows travel, hospitality, and confusion. It does not show authority to impose a commercial debt on the firm.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Mr. Swine's feelings about the invoice are vivid. I will translate them into evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The tribunal need not decide whether Mr. Swine was wise. Only whether the claimant has proven the obligation it invoices. It has not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You hid support for pro-bono cases inside ski expenses. That is either noble, reckless, or a new accounting tort.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The firm survived because the contract was not a contract. I will try not to make that our business model.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A notice to vacate unless we prove continuing legal-social utility. That is an insult with filing instructions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Mr. Pig, the city is not complimenting us. It is attempting to close us in administrative language.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: These are not abstract consequences. These are people trying to understand why a printer has opinions about their homes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Tell me what the notice does to you, not what the notice says it does. The Court will need both.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Everything here is polished enough to deny responsibility.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: It says access to justice hub while removing the people providing access to justice. Ambitious, in the way sinkholes are ambitious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Then every individual appears to have received the same printer settings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The contractor profits when the noticed people disappear. Paper usually leads to money; today it is sprinting.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This is not office equipment. This is bureaucracy with ammunition.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Objections are not confetti. Stop shredding them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The print run is stopped, the logs are intact, and I have only moderate paper trauma.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: We stopped the machine. The evidence is bruised, but still testifying.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: This ledger is not luxury spending. It is a map of cases that would have disappeared without help.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: You saved the firm by behaving exactly like someone destroying it. That is not a defence strategy I recommend repeating.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A final brief is not a pile of good points. It is the route through them. We assign people where they can prove something.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Mr. Pig can explain why the firm matters. He should not explain procurement unless the Court asks about dread.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Bajtek explains the filter. I argue why hiding discretion inside automation is still discretion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Individual impact needs an individual voice. The Court should not hear only the city's summary of the people it ignored.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: A notice that no ordinary recipient can understand is not notice. It is decoration with consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Public interest is not a spell that makes individual lives disappear.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Automation did not remove discretion. It hid it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The same system that produced the notices directs profit toward the people who benefit from silence. That is not neutral administration.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The question is not whether the city may modernise. It may. The question is whether modernisation may begin by silencing the people most likely to need law.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Suspend the scheme, restore the objections, require individual assessment, preserve access to legal aid, and review the procurement process openly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The city has hurt the printer's feelings and should apologise in writing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: The office is still shabby. That may be the point. People came through the door because it looked like a place that might let them in.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Dr. A. Cula
text: Pig & Swine remains open. Not because it is efficient. Because it is reachable.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.
